one_line: Young Gupta-age astronomer-mathematician who measures the cosmos and declares the turning Earth.

appearance.age_look: Early twenties, lean and wiry with a scholar’s light musculature from walking and temple duties.
appearance.face: Oval face with warm medium‑brown complexion, sharp straight nose, high cheekbones, a small tilak mark of red sandal paste on the forehead, calm dark eyes that widen with curiosity, default expression a focused, faintly smiling intensity.
appearance.hair: Straight black hair parted in the middle, drawn back into a smooth, tight topknot at the rear crown (small, compact knot), sides neatly oiled and combed, with no loose strands; no headgear, but a simple thin copper circlet sits low around the topknot base during study or ritual.
appearance.costume: Bare upper torso except for a finely woven off‑white cotton uttarīya (shawl) with a thin maroon border draped from left shoulder across the chest and around the back, worn over a knee‑length unstitched dhoti of soft undyed cream cotton with a narrow deep‑indigo stripe along the hem, pleated in front and tied at the waist; he wears a narrow brown leather girdle over the dhoti knot to tuck small palm‑leaf slips, simple dark wooden bead mala around the neck, a single thin copper armlet on the right upper arm, and plain leather sandals on his feet; no extravagant jewellery, overall an austere Gupta‑era Brahmin scholar look from Magadha.
appearance.signature: A palm‑leaf manuscript bundle of the Aryabhatiya tied with a thin cord, held with one hand and often accompanied by a small hand‑held gnomon (shadow stick) of dark wood tucked into his girdle.

ref_desc: Young lean Āryabhaṭa with medium‑brown oval face, compact black topknot, red sandal tilak, off‑white uttarīya over a cream dhoti with deep‑indigo hem stripe, wooden mala, copper armlet, leather sandals, and a tied palm‑leaf Aryabhatiya manuscript with dark‑wood gnomon, in off‑white, maroon and indigo tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full‑body anime‑style model sheet of a young Gupta‑era Indian scholar: lean early‑twenties build, medium‑brown oval face, sharp nose, high cheekbones, calm dark eyes and a small red sandalwood tilak on his forehead, with straight black hair neatly oiled and pulled back into a compact topknot at the rear crown, encircled by a thin copper band; no loose hair. Show him bare‑chested except for a finely woven off‑white cotton shawl with a thin maroon border (uttarīya) draped from his left shoulder across his chest and around his back, over a knee‑length cream cotton dhoti with a narrow deep‑indigo stripe at the hem, carefully pleated in front and tied at the waist. Add a narrow brown leather belt over the dhoti knot where a small dark‑wood hand gnomon can be tucked, a plain wooden bead mala around his neck, a thin copper armlet on his right upper arm, and simple brown leather sandals on his feet, with no other jewellery to keep his look austere and scholarly. His dominant colours should be off‑white, cream, maroon, deep indigo and brown. Place a tied bundle of palm‑leaf manuscripts of the Aryabhatiya in one hand as his constant prop, and pose him neutrally so all costume details, hair, face and the manuscript with gnomon are clearly visible for consistent redrawing.
